description_long: This is a short section of a white wooden picket fence, featuring four vertical pickets with pointed tops and a slightly irregular alignment. The pickets are attached to two horizontal rails, which are also painted white and show subtle signs of weathering and discoloration, particularly near the base where a greenish tint suggests contact with grass or soil. The pickets vary slightly in height and angle, giving the fence a rustic, handmade appearance. The surface of the wood is matte with visible grain and some minor imperfections, enhancing its natural look. There are no visible decorative elements or post caps beyond the pointed tops of the pickets. This type of fence is commonly found in residential yards, gardens, or parks, serving as a low barrier or boundary marker.
description: A short, white wooden picket fence with four pointed pickets and two horizontal rails, showing slight weathering and a rustic appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing four pointed pickets with uneven heights and two horizontal rails, with visible weathering at the base.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence and the alignment of the pickets and rails.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the pickets and rails, with the same weathered look and greenish discoloration at the bottom.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the fence and the staggered picket arrangement.
